title: Clinic referral and transport notebook
summary: Matches sick or injured people with the safest available route to care.
content: Clinic referral and transport notebook is a practical offline routine for health transport. List clinics, pharmacies, first-aid points, ambulance access points, and people with medical training if known. Record each referral need: person, urgency, reason, mobility, documents, medicines, escort, and destination. Match transport to the person's condition; do not send severe cases alone. Keep a return-check time so no patient disappears from the system. Update routes after curfew, flood, roadblock, fuel shortage, or security changes. The goal is to make the action repeatable by a tired person, visible to helpers, and safe enough to stop when official help or a safer option appears.
materials: Notebook. Context: “Clinic referral and transport notebook”.
Contact list. Context: “Clinic referral and transport notebook”.
steps: List clinics, pharmacies, first-aid points, ambulance access points, and people with medical training if known.
Record each referral need: person, urgency, reason, mobility, documents, medicines, escort, and destination.
Match transport to the person's condition; do not send severe cases alone.
Keep a return-check time so no patient disappears from the system.
Update routes after curfew, flood, roadblock, fuel shortage, or security changes.
warnings: Use Clinic referral and transport notebook as practical support, not as a replacement for official guidance, trained help, or evacuation when needed.
Protect privacy and infection-control boundaries, but never let paperwork delay lifesaving action in “Clinic referral and transport notebook”.
common_mistakes: Starting from memory instead of written notes while carrying out “Clinic referral and transport notebook”
Letting one tired person control the whole process while carrying out “Clinic referral and transport notebook”
Ignoring the review time while carrying out “Clinic referral and transport notebook”
alternatives: Use a wall board when many people are involved as a fallback for “Clinic referral and transport notebook”
Use pocket cards when movement is required as a fallback for “Clinic referral and transport notebook”
failure_signs: People disagree about the facts, the plan is not being followed, or the situation worsens after the first review during “Clinic referral and transport notebook”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Clinic referral and transport notebook to delay emergency care, evacuation, fire response, or official instructions.
short_term: Write the first safe action, responsible person, materials, and next review time. This note applies specifically to Clinic referral and transport notebook.
long_term: Convert the routine into a repeatable checklist with handover notes and backups. This note applies specifically to Clinic referral and transport notebook.
if_method_fails: Reduce complexity, stop unsafe work, and switch to the safest available fallback. This note applies specifically to Clinic referral and transport notebook.
environment_notes: Adapt the routine to heat, cold, crowding, darkness, limited water, and local hazards. This note applies specifically to Clinic referral and transport notebook.
